results.title: Modal: The serverless cloud infra for AI, ML, and data applications | Product Hunt
results.content:
Pros

inference at scale(4)

training pipelines(3)

cold start management(2)

fine-tuning models(2)

Python hosting(2)

scalable GPU serving(2)

Viktor for Microsoft Teams

used Modal to build Viktor for Microsoft Teams

•9 reviews

Viktor runs its agents server-side, 24/7, which means spinning up isolated sandboxes for thousands of teams at once and scaling back to zero the moment they go idle. We looked at rolling our own on Kubernetes and at the usual serverless suspects. Modal was the only one where a Python engineer could define the whole sandbox, schedule, and scaling story in code and ship it the same afternoon. It is a big part of why a small team can run this much compute without standing up a platform team to babysit it.

#### Alternatives Considered [...] Launched on January 22nd, 2025

Modal

ModalYour end-to-end stack for cloud compute

Launched on November 16th, 2022

5.0

Based on 53 reviews

Review Modal?

Reviewers are uniformly positive, though the feedback comes almost entirely from founders using Modal in production. The clearest pattern is fast spin-up, low infrastructure overhead, and a strong Python-first developer experience for heavy compute, sandboxes, and GPU jobs. Makers of Fathom, Kodezi, and viktor.com describe it as core infrastructure that lets small teams ship training, inference, isolated sandboxes, and scheduled workloads without building or managing their own platform.

results.title: viktor.com: The AI employee that does the work, in Slack & Teams
results.content:
#### vs Alternatives

Before Viktor, we explored tools like Zapier, Make, Slack Workflow Builder, and custom scripts. Each worked for simple or structured tasks, but broke down when workflows became more dynamic or required real decision making. Traditional automation tools were too rigid, and standalone AI tools could not take real action within our systems. Viktor stood out because it combines reasoning with execution directly inside Slack, allowing us to handle complex workflows without needing to build and maintain our own infrastructure.

New User

#### What's great

It's all of the contextual awareness and action-taking capability you would want in an AI assistant with zero setup headache.

#### What needs improvement [...] #### What needs improvement

There are a few cases where it will respond to a message twice. I think there's a massive opportunity in having the tool ship with some preloaded frameworks around consolidating information around the rules you've given it to follow.

#### vs Alternatives

I've previously used another platform called Lindi, and while I loved that it worked in iMessage, its interface and overall workflow building experience were less than ideal. With Victor, setting up new flows and building new tools is ridiculously straightforward, and it works phenomenally with your team.

PACT 
Zack Fediay

#### What's great [...] 108

480

Jordan Dikoum

#### What's great

Viktor has become a core part of how we run operations at UniTru, giving us leverage that would normally require engineering resources. It allows us to manage workflows across Slack and Google Sheets seamlessly, handling things like scheduling, status tracking, and coordination in real time. What stands out most is how quickly we can build, test, and refine workflows without slowing down, which is critical for a fast moving team. At a high level, Viktor does not just save time, it enables us to operate at a level of speed, organization, and scale that would not be possible otherwise.

#### What needs improvement
